concerns.text: Data collection extends to crossing number ≤13 but validation limited to ≤10. Plan states 'Phase 1 conclusions are explicitly limited to validated crossing number ≤10 data' yet regression modeling and analysis sections describe using the full dataset. This creates methodological ambiguity: which data supports which conclusions? Specify whether regression models are fit on validated subset only or full dataset, and whether conclusions are qualified accordingly.

The composite complexity score is acknowledged as exploratory with no theoretical basis (FR-006), yet treated as a validation target in FR-007 and SC-003. The plan doesn't establish why this weighted combination should predict hyperbolic volume better than individual invariants. Add theoretical justification or reframe as purely exploratory comparison without validation claims.

Multiple testing inflation not addressed. With 3 regression models, dual correlations (Pearson + Spearman), ANOVA across groups, and multiple invariants computed, Type I error risk increases. The Assumptions section states 'no multiple comparison correction applied unless findings suggest otherwise' but this should be a methodology decision, not post-hoc. Specify correction method (e.g., Bonferroni, FDR) or justify no correction.

Hyperbolic volume filtering excludes torus/satellite knots (FR-014), creating selection bias. While acknowledged in Assumptions, the plan doesn't quantify the excluded proportion or assess impact on generalizability. Add documentation of excluded knot count/percentage and discuss whether conclusions apply only to hyperbolic knots.

ANOVA applied to discrete integer-valued invariants (crossing number, braid index) with small ranges. While robust alternatives (Welch's, Kruskal-Wallis) are mentioned, the fundamental limitation of parametric tests on discrete data with limited integer values isn't fully addressed. Specify minimum crossing number range required for ANOVA validity or use non-parametric tests as primary.
